process_name: Exclusive radiative b -> d gamma decays
implementation_difficulty_reason: Requires new b -> d gamma electromagnetic/chromomagnetic dipole matching, C7/C7' and C8/C8' RG treatment, exclusive B -> rho/omega form-factor and annihilation inputs, and a likelihood for branching fractions, isospin, CP, and CKM-ratio observables.
priority_rationale: Deferred by DA-4 (round_004_addendum_deferred_scope.md); b -> d gamma exclusive tail, CKM-suppressed relative to B011/B012. Promoted in Wave-8 because: Top-tier; provides down-sector dipole chirality probe distinct from b -> s gamma. Lower implementation priority than Waves 1-7 PRIMARY entries.

grep_commands: rg -n "rho.?gamma|rho\s*\+?\s*gamma|omega.?gamma|b.?->.?d.?gamma|b.?to.?d.?gamma|X_d.?gamma|Xd.?gamma|B.?->.?rho|B.?->.?omega|B014" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "C7'?|C_7'?|O7'?|C8'?|C_8'?|O8'?|dipole|photon.?polar|right.?handed.?dipole|radiative" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused B014 grep for B -> rho gamma, B -> omega gamma, b -> d gamma, Xd gamma, and B014 returned exit=1 with no matches.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 defines a lepton-sector mu -> e gamma dipole check, not a quark b -> d gamma implementation.
flavorConstraints/README.md:3 documents the brane-localized dipole NDA estimate for the lepton flavor module only.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 in the modern quark policy surface.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903, :922, and :941 implement B_d, B_s, and D0 mixing evaluators, not radiative b -> d gamma decays.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(B\to\rho\gamma\), \(B\to\omega\gamma\).
This entry covers the exclusive radiative \(b\to d\gamma\) channels
\(B^+\to\rho^+\gamma\), \(B^0\to\rho^0\gamma\), and
\(B^0\to\omega\gamma\), plus their common HFLAV \(B\to\rho\gamma\) and
\(B\to\rho/\omega\gamma\) averages where useful.  The process is CKM
suppressed relative to the \(b\to s\gamma\) entries B011/B012 and probes the
down-sector electromagnetic dipole with different flavor insertions.
sections.pdg_prose:
The PDG 2025 listings give the exclusive component averages
\[
  {\cal B}(B^+\to\rho^+\gamma)=(9.8\pm2.5)\times10^{-7},
\]
\[
  {\cal B}(B^0\to\rho^0\gamma)=(8.6\pm1.5)\times10^{-7},
\qquad
  {\cal B}(B^0\to\omega\gamma)=
    \left(4.4^{+1.8}_{-1.6}\right)\times10^{-7}.
\]
The HFLAV end-of-year 2024 rare-decay table gives, in units of \(10^{-6}\),
\[
  {\cal B}(B\to\rho\gamma)=1.40\pm0.22,\qquad
  {\cal B}(B\to\rho/\omega\gamma)=1.30\pm0.18,
\]
and quotes the naive isospin average
\[
  \frac{\Gamma(B^+\to\rho^+\gamma)}
       {2\Gamma(B^0\to\rho^0\gamma)}-1=-0.46\pm0.17.
\]
HFLAV explicitly cautions that the isospin averages in that table are computed
naively.  The sidecar also records the HFLAV semi-inclusive
\(B\to X_d\gamma\) average, \((9.2\pm3.0)\times10^{-6}\), as a related
normalization and CKM-ratio input, not as the exclusive B014 headline value.
All values are backed by local snapshots under
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B014/}.
sections.relevance:
This Wave-8 entry is \textbf{SECONDARY} under
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/PRIORITY\_TIERS.md}.  In an RS/anarchic-flavor
treatment the relevant short-distance objects are the \(b\to d\)
electromagnetic and chromomagnetic dipole coefficients \(C_7^{(d)}\),
\(C_7^{\prime(d)}\), \(C_8^{(d)}\), and \(C_8^{\prime(d)}\).  KK gauge bosons,
KK fermions, and Higgs-sector loops can induce chirality-flipped dipoles whose
amplitudes depend on 5D fermion localization and anarchic Yukawa insertions.
Compared with B011/B012, this entry tests the same dipole machinery in the
\(b\to d\) flavor direction, where the exclusive isospin and CP observables can
be sensitive to right-handed photons, spectator effects, and weak-annihilation
contamination.
sections.post_2008:
BaBar arXiv:1005.4087 extended the 2008 Belle/BaBar exclusive picture by
studying semi-inclusive \(B\to X_{s,d}\gamma\) and extracting
\(|V_{td}/V_{ts}|\).  Hurth and Nakao arXiv:1005.1224 is the compact
post-2008 review of how inclusive and exclusive \(b\to d\gamma\) data are
compared with \(b\to s\gamma\).

Belle/Belle II arXiv:2407.08984 provides a newer joint \(B\to\rho\gamma\)
measurement with CP and isospin asymmetries, while LHCb arXiv:2507.14401
reports a high-precision \(B^0\to\rho(770)^0\gamma\) branching fraction
normalized to \(B^0\to K^{*0}\gamma\).  These are important for a future
likelihood update even though the canonical PDG/HFLAV snapshot values above
remain the conservative catalog anchors.

Descotes-Genon et al. arXiv:1104.3342 gives the standard \(C_7\)-\(C_7'\)
language for radiative \(B\) constraints, and Bharucha--Straub--Zwicky
arXiv:1503.05534 supplies updated \(B\to\rho\) and \(B\to\omega\) form-factor
inputs from light-cone sum rules.  These sources matter because an exclusive
\(B\to\rho/\omega\gamma\) constraint is only as clean as its dipole matching,
RG evolution, and form-factor/annihilation prescription.
sections.validity:
Class: useful but model-dependent.  The inclusive \(b\to d\gamma\) rate and
the exclusive \(B\to\rho/\omega\gamma\) averages constrain \(|C_7^{(d)}|^2\)
and \(|C_7^{\prime(d)}|^2\) combinations only after choosing CKM inputs,
exclusive form factors, weak-annihilation treatment, and a convention for SM
long-distance pieces.  The isospin asymmetry should be treated as diagnostic
unless the implementation includes spectator and annihilation effects.  The
LHCb 2025 measurement also carries an explicit saturation assumption for the
selected \(\rho(770)^0\) and \(K^{*}(892)^0\) dihadron spectra.  A hard RS
exclusion should therefore come from a dedicated Wilson-coefficient likelihood,
not from a direct comparison of raw branching fractions alone.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  A focused search across \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} for \(B\to\rho\gamma\),
\(B\to\omega\gamma\), \(b\to d\gamma\), \(X_d\gamma\), and B014 returned no
matches.  Broader dipole searches find only lepton-sector coverage, for
example \texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75} and
\texttt{flavorConstraints/README.md:3}.  The modern quark policy surface at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} enumerates only
\(\varepsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) mixing systems; the
\(\Delta F=2\) beauty routines at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903},
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922}, and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941} do not implement radiative
\(b\to d\gamma\) decays.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  The repo would need new \(b\to d\gamma\) dipole matching,
running and mixing for \(C_7^{(d)}\), \(C_7^{\prime(d)}\), \(C_8^{(d)}\), and
\(C_8^{\prime(d)}\), a bridge from RS overlap/Yukawa data into those
coefficients, and an exclusive \(B\to\rho/\omega\gamma\) likelihood with
form-factor, isospin, CP, and CKM-ratio uncertainties.  Existing
\(\Delta F=2\) and \(\mu\to e\gamma\) code cannot be reused as a finished
constraint.

\texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor}.

L18:   The result assumes that rho(770)0 and K*(892)0 decays saturate the dihadron mass spectra considered in the analysis.
L19:   The abstract describes the result as consistent with the current world average and by far the most precise measurement to date.

priority_rationale_short: Secondary b->d gamma check, CKM and form-factor limited
priority_rationale_full: Exclusive b -> d gamma modes test dipole flavor structure in the b-d direction, which is relevant but not usually the leading RS pressure point. The bounds require CKM inputs, form factors, weak-annihilation treatment, and exclusive-amplitude assumptions. They are secondary diagnostics dominated by the cleaner mixing and inclusive radiative constraints.
